summary: Set 044 Session 5 CLOSED. Cross-provider consensus completed (Pass A + Pass B per workflow.md §Prompt-framing discipline); proposal.md v1 published as the post-consensus architectural commitment for log-harvest observability; Set 045 spec stub created; Sets 037-041 cancelled with audit-history CANCELLED.md + state-file status flipped to 'cancelled' per Set 035's sole-truth migration. (1) Pre-S5 hygiene: discarded uncommitted Codex/gpt-5.4 check-out from 2026-05-23T04:08 that produced no committed work; ran start_session under claude/anthropic/claude-opus-4-7/high per the S4 disposition's recommendation. (2) Ran the deferred Copilot effort sidebar (project_044_copilot_effort_sidebar_deferred memory) BEFORE proposal drafting per operator direction. Q3 CLOSED: gen_ai.request.reasoning_effort is OMITTED at low + medium (S4a) + high — Branch A is empirically dead on Copilot. Q4 PARTIALLY ANSWERED: per-turn reasoning.output_tokens does NOT distinguish low from medium; per-session aggregate volume separates high from not-high but underpowered at N=1. Material implication: both backends symmetric in lacking native A3, simplifying one axis of the proposal. Zero router cost. (3) Drafted proposal.md v0 (~290 lines): headline 'build harvest, retire 037-041'; S6 ships a Copilot-only MVP; Set 045 owns Claude parser + joiner. Operator floated 'Python launch wrapper as logging interceptor' idea mid-draft (.NET pipeline analogy). Added as §8.7 explicit open question (NOT baked into headline). (4) Pass A consensus call across three providers at max effort (gpt-5-4, gemini-pro, claude-opus-4-6) with the canonical bias-cautions preamble: $0.804 total ($0.158 + $0.044 + $0.602). All three ENDORSE-WITH-REVISIONS the harvest direction. 3/3 convergence: promote §8.7 wrapper to primary, narration to fallback; drop per-turn narration permanently; wrapper in ai_router/; headless-first; ungated-default; forward-compatible with 042-043. New risks raised by all three: log-format schema drift, sensitive-content retention, workspace-identity ambiguity, wrapper-bypass. Material disagreement: S6 vote split 2 GO / 1 NO-GO; joiner location split 2 Python / 1 TypeScript. (5) Pass B devil's-advocate steelman of three specific contrarian claims (A: wrapper is launch-adapters-in-disguise; B: narration-as-fallback trivializes the real adoption story; C: S6 ships producer without consumer): $0.647 total ($0.117 + $0.040 + $0.491). Material shifts: 3/3 acknowledged that proposal v0 §8.7 framing biased Pass A; corrected to 'dual-primary, two co-equal channels' (wrapper + narration both production-grade). S6 vote FLIPPED from 2 GO / 1 NO-GO to 2 NO-GO / 1 conditional-GO: gemini-pro flipped to NO-GO citing schema-commitment-without-joiner; gpt-5-4 strengthened NO-GO; opus-4-6 stayed GO only with revised scope adding narration v1.1 as co-primary. (6) Per operator approval of Option A + Sub-option 2 (dual-primary, NO-GO S6, write Set 045 spec stub now): revised proposal to v1 (folded wrapper INTO §4 architecture as Channel 2; DEFER on S6 implementation; narration v1.1 as co-equal not fallback; §10 audit section citing both passes + framing-bias finding). Authored proposal-consensus-journal.md as companion audit trail. (7) Created Set 045 spec stub (6 sessions, status not-started) locking the architectural commitments. Authored CANCELLED.md for Sets 037-041 each citing Set 044 proposal v1; flipped each set's session-state.json status to 'cancelled'. (8) Cumulative Set 044 routed spend across S1-S5: $1.7442 of $15.00 NTE (well under budget). Framing-bias finding documented in consensus-journal §6 as a recommendation back to workflow.md: promote two-pass-default for roadmap-reshape decisions from 'Yes if budget allows' to 'Yes always.'

reason.specifics: Session 6 is the conditional implementation + close-out session per spec.md. The S5 consensus locked DEFER on in-set S6 implementation (2 of 3 Pass B NO-GO), so S6 is a NON-IMPLEMENTING close-out session: it should NOT produce wrapper code, parser shims, or Explorer changes. S6 should: (a) write change-log.md summarizing what 044 actually shipped (documents only — proposal v1, consensus journal, sidebar results, Set 045 spec stub, Sets 037-041 cancellations); (b) verify all artifacts are committed and the disposition + activity-log are complete; (c) run the canonical close-out sequence (python -m ai_router.close_session with manual-verify attestation citing the Pass A + Pass B verification message IDs); (d) the per-spec uat-checklist is conditional on 'S5 decided implement' which it did not — degrade to a close-out summary per spec.md S6 step 3. S6 should NOT call start_session under a different orchestrator without operator approval since trajectory continuity matters for the close-out narrative. S6 budget envelope: trivial — no routed calls expected unless an end-of-set verification round is added; Set 044 cumulative spend $1.7442 of $15.00 NTE leaves $13.26 unused. S6 HANDOFF GOTCHAS: (i) change-log.md must pre-exist before close_session per memory project_final_session_changelog_pre_close (gate fires before the idempotent writes). (ii) The 045-log-harvest-implementation directory was created during S5; ensure it appears correctly in the Session Set Explorer post-S6-close. (iii) The Sets 037-041 CANCELLED.md files + flipped status fields are the Set 035 sole-truth-cancellation shape; the Explorer should display them as cancelled in the cancelled bucket. (iv) The disposition.json field 'next_orchestrator.engine' identifies who runs S6; if operator wants a different orchestrator (e.g., Codex), they should override at S6 start_session. (v) Optionally, S6 can run a single small routed verification call (~$0.05) to independently confirm the proposal-consensus-journal.md captured both passes accurately, but this is OPTIONAL — the Pass A + Pass B verdicts are already verifications-by-routing and stand on their own.
